instructions: Resolve every required input before side effects.
Stop when a required capability is denied, unsupported, or awaiting approval.
Stay within the project or an explicitly approved artifact root.
Preserve the named output contract and report skipped validation honestly.
Execute only declared workflow effects after satisfying their authorization states (workspace-read:preapproved); authorization alone never expands effect scope.

behaviorContract.purpose: Perform standard evidence-grounded review and group findings by severity without implementation.

inputs.name: REVIEW_SCOPE
inputs.description: Code, design, tests, logs, or diff to review.

when.condition: A finding can cause data loss, security or financial harm, production instability, or incorrect core behavior.
A finding materially affects correctness, maintainability, scalability, or realistic bug risk.
A finding is low-risk but affects clarity, consistency, or best practice.

invariants: Do not write code or full implementation examples.
Do not approve, reject, or decide release readiness.
stopConditions: Evidence needed for a finding is unavailable.

workflowSteps.action: Review correctness, complexity, performance, concurrency, errors, tests, and maintainability.
Separate observed facts from inference and assumptions.
Describe issue, impact, and directional improvement.

deviationPolicy.instructions: Do not expand scope, redesign the system, or implement fixes.

validation: Confirm every finding includes issue, impact, and directional improvement.
Confirm severity follows the decision table.
Confirm no implementation or release decision appears.

claimBoundary: Behavior-complete machine-readable contract generated from the canonical behavior IR. Workflow-level effects are authoritative; behaviorContract.effects declares the validated empty subset and cannot expand workflow capability scope. The referenced authored guidance surface may add explanatory context but cannot override this contract.
